data.task:
Phase 1, atom 1 of componentizing packages/core/tests/runner.test.ts (currently 5,638 lines / 151 tests). Goal: establish the shared test-support module and prove the file-split pattern on the two SMALL top-level describe blocks only, leaving the large runRun block in place for later atoms.

Do exactly this:
1. Create packages/core/tests/runner.test-support.ts (a non-test helper module, NOT a *.test.ts file). Move into it ONLY the shared setup currently at the top of runner.test.ts that is used by more than one describe: the `persona`/`oscar`/`bob`/`deb` builders, the shared `priority`/`workspaceRoot` fixtures, and every shared helper function/constant/factory the tests call. Export each one. Keep the production imports from '../src/index.js' and '../src/runner/founder-stop.js' that these helpers need. Do NOT move test bodies.
2. In runner.test.ts, replace the moved definitions with named imports from './runner.test-support.js'.
3. Create packages/core/tests/parse-triage.test.ts and move the `describe('parseTriage', ...)` block (lines ~675-698) into it, importing what it needs from './runner.test-support.js' and the relevant vitest/src imports.
4. Create packages/core/tests/founder-closeout.test.ts and move the `describe('founder closeout target-aware Run Status', ...)` block (lines ~699-824) into it the same way.
5. Leave `describe('runRun (multi-atom loop)', ...)` in runner.test.ts for now.

Acceptance criteria (must all hold):
- `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core test` is GREEN with the SAME total test count (151) — show the vitest summary as evidence.
- `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core run typecheck` passes.
- NO test assertion, expectation, name, or `it/test` body is changed, skipped, or deleted — this is a pure move/extract. The diff must be relocation + import wiring only.
- Shared helpers/fixtures now live in ONE owner module (runner.test-support.ts); no helper is duplicated across files.

Write scope: packages/core/tests/**. Do not touch product code under packages/core/src/** or any other package. If you find a helper that is genuinely used by only one describe, keep it co-located in that describe's file rather than the shared module. Report the final line counts of each touched/created file and the vitest summary.

data.reason: Pure move: shared fixtures extracted to one runner.test-support.ts owner; parseTriage and founder-closeout describes split into their own files; full @cocoder/core suite green (741 passed, 0 failed/skipped), typecheck clean, no assertion/skip changes.

data.task:
Phase 1, atom 2 of componentizing packages/core/tests/runner.test.ts (now 5,341 lines). This is a FOUNDATION atom: move the remaining shared test scaffolding into the one support module so later atoms can split the runRun tests across files. Do NOT move any test bodies this atom.

Move ALL of the top-level helper definitions currently between the import block and the `describe('runRun (multi-atom loop)', ...)` line (≈ lines 54-526 of runner.test.ts) into packages/core/tests/runner.test-support.ts, and export each one. This is the shared scaffolding the runRun tests use, including: fakeSessionHost, worktreeStubs, scriptedGit, recordingScriptedGit, okAdapter, fakeIO, doneJudge, delegate, askFounderContinue, writePathDelegate, loopDelegate, wrapup, sleep, gatedStallHarness, writeFounderStopSignal, settledCommitState, settledCloseout, baseDeps, recordingWindowGit, ticketIndex, emptyTicketIndex, ticketMarkdown, makeTicketWorkspace, runInputFor, runTestsPlaySources, the shared `input` and `stopFaultEvents` consts, and any local type aliases these depend on (e.g. RecordedCommit). Move helper-private types/constants they reference too, and add whatever production imports from '../src/index.js' / '../src/runner/founder-stop.js' the moved helpers need (Adapter, Git, RunnerDeps, SpawnOptions, etc.).

Then in runner.test.ts, replace the removed definitions with named imports from './runner.test-support.js', leaving the `describe('runRun (multi-atom loop)', ...)` block and its test bodies exactly as-is. Prune any now-unused imports from runner.test.ts's '../src/index.js' import list (and add them to the support module instead).

Acceptance criteria (all must hold):
- `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core test` GREEN with the same total package test count and zero skips — show the vitest summary.
- `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core run typecheck` passes.
- Pure move/relocation: NO test body, assertion, name, or `it/test` call is changed, skipped, or deleted. No `.only/.skip/.todo` introduced.
- One owner: every shared helper lives in runner.test-support.ts; none is duplicated. runner.test.ts no longer defines these helpers, only imports them.

Write scope: packages/core/tests/** only. Do not touch packages/core/src/** or other packages. Report the new line count of runner.test.ts and runner.test-support.ts and the vitest summary.

data.reason: Foundation move: all shared runRun scaffolding relocated to the single runner.test-support.ts owner, runRun block untouched; full @cocoder/core suite green (741 passed, 0 failed/skipped), typecheck clean, no test/assertion/skip changes.

data.task:
Phase 1, atom 3 of componentizing packages/core/tests/runner.test.ts. Begin splitting the big `describe('runRun (multi-atom loop)', ...)` block by theme. Extract these FOUR contiguous test clusters, each into its own new file under packages/core/tests/, moving the test bodies VERBATIM. Each cluster is a contiguous run of `test(...)` blocks inside the runRun describe; move from the named first test through the named last test, inclusive.

1. packages/core/tests/runner-launch-model.test.ts — from test 'missing Objective launches with required questions for priority repair' THROUGH test 'cmux group label derives compatibility targets when RunInput.target is absent' (launch + model-assignment cluster, 7 tests).
2. packages/core/tests/runner-ticket-close.test.ts — from test 'run-wrap audit FLAGS a commit that advanced HEAD outside the run ledger (ADR-0041 §4 / 0058)' THROUGH test 'in-run ticket close commit failure fails loudly' (wrap-audit + in-run ticket-close cluster, 8 tests).
3. packages/core/tests/runner-scope-advisory.test.ts — from test 'does not infer hard scope conflicts from directive prose' THROUGH test 'does not treat reference paths and ignore globs as required writes' (advisory-scope / out-of-lane prose cluster, 7 tests).
4. packages/core/tests/runner-onboard-spend.test.ts — from test 'onboard-existing audit recon writes are in-scope while product writes are still hard-refused' THROUGH test 'ordinary priorities commit out-of-lane atom files without tripping the audit boundary' (onboard-existing spend-gate cluster, 8 tests).

For each new file: wrap the moved tests in `describe('runRun (multi-atom loop) — <short theme>', () => { ... })` (the only new text allowed is this wrapper describe label; do NOT change any `test(...)` title, body, or assertion). Import every helper the moved tests use from './runner.test-support.js', plus needed vitest primitives (describe, test, expect) and any '../src/index.js' / founder-stop imports those tests reference.

Leave all OTHER runRun tests (the core multi-atom-loop tests and everything from 'rebuilds the Oz UI bundle' onward) in runner.test.ts untouched. After removing the four clusters, prune any now-unused imports from runner.test.ts.

Acceptance criteria (all must hold):
- `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core test` GREEN with the SAME total package test count and ZERO skips — show the vitest summary.
- `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core run typecheck` passes.
- Pure move: no `test(...)` title, body, assertion, or expectation changed/skipped/deleted; no `.only/.skip/.todo` introduced. Only new text is the four wrapper `describe` labels and import lines.
- Each moved helper is imported from the single runner.test-support.ts owner; no helper duplicated into the new files.

Write scope: packages/core/tests/** only. Report the line counts of runner.test.ts and the four new files, plus the vitest summary.

data.reason: Four runRun clusters (launch/model, ticket-close, scope-advisory, onboard-spend) extracted verbatim into own files importing the support owner; full suite green (741 passed, 0 failed/skipped, now 85 files), typecheck clean, no assertion/skip changes.

Leave ALL other runRun tests in runner.test.ts untouched (the core multi-atom-loop tests, the founder-stop/held-resume cluster, the wrap-up Play cluster, the caps/nudges cluster, the Deb triage/watchdog/repair cluster, and preflight). After removing the five clusters, prune any now-unused imports from runner.test.ts.

Acceptance criteria (all must hold):
- `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core test` GREEN with the SAME total package test count and ZERO skips — show the vitest summary.
- `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core run typecheck` passes.
- Pure move: no `test(...)` title, body, assertion, or expectation changed/skipped/deleted; no `.only/.skip/.todo` introduced. Only new text is the five wrapper `describe` labels and import lines.
- Each moved helper is imported from the single runner.test-support.ts owner; no helper duplicated.

Write scope: packages/core/tests/** only. Report the line count of runner.test.ts and the five new files plus the vitest summary.

The prior attempt was rejected because it added a brand-new test.each to the already-committed runner-launch-model.test.ts and dropped 2 tests, changing nothing in the global count. THIS MUST BE A PURE MOVE AND NOTHING ELSE.

HARD CONSTRAINTS:
- Do NOT create, add, rename, consolidate, parametrize, or delete ANY test. No new test.each. No merging two tests into one. Move existing `test(...)` blocks VERBATIM.
- Do NOT modify ANY existing committed file other than runner.test.ts. Specifically do NOT touch runner-launch-model.test.ts, runner-scope-advisory.test.ts, runner.test-support.ts, or any other existing test file. The ONLY files you may change are: the three NEW files below, and runner.test.ts (from which you remove the moved blocks and prune now-unused imports).

1. packages/core/tests/runner-deb-triage.test.ts — from 'Deb-backed watchdog nudges an idle Oscar while awaiting a directive only when Deb is present' THROUGH 'tracks Oz and Deb nudge seqs independently across their runner delivery loops'.
2. packages/core/tests/runner-deb-repair.test.ts — from 'repair mode commits only Deb-declared repair files when filesChanged is present' THROUGH 'a recurring fault escalates on the 2nd occurrence: Deb files a ticket, gate-committed (ADR-0016 §recurrence)'.
3. packages/core/tests/runner-preflight.test.ts — from 'preflight failure aborts before spawning and marks the run failed' THROUGH 'onRunCreated fires synchronously with the created run (daemon learns runId for its 202)'.

For each new file: wrap the moved tests in `describe('runRun (multi-atom loop) — <short theme>', () => { ... })` (the ONLY new text allowed anywhere is these three wrapper describe labels and the new files' import lines). Import every helper the moved tests use from './runner.test-support.js', plus needed vitest primitives and any '../src/index.js' / founder-stop imports those tests reference.

After extraction, runner.test.ts must contain ONLY the two core multi-atom-loop tests ('drives Bob through MULTIPLE atoms...' and 'surfaces a mid-run founder decision as held...'), untouched, inside the runRun describe; prune its now-unused imports.

data.reason: Clean pure move: Deb-triage, Deb-repair, preflight clusters extracted verbatim into 3 new files; only runner.test.ts + the 3 new files changed (launch-model untouched), global count exactly 741, typecheck clean, no skips. runner.test.ts now holds only the 2 core loop tests.

data.question:
Phase 1 is done: runner.test.ts (5,638 lines, one monolithic describe) is now 17 focused files + one shared runner.test-support.ts fixture owner, with 741 tests green and zero behavior change throughout. Phase 2 asks us to 'decide a better test architecture going forward' — a governance standard I should not set unilaterally, so I need your call.

My research: the repo ALREADY follows 'one cohesive behavior area per test file' (96 small core test files); runner.test.ts was the lone outlier, and the split just brought it back into line. There is no written test-architecture standard today. So the gap isn't a new architecture — it's codifying the convention that already works, plus a trigger that prevents the next monolith.

Recommended standard (4 rules): (1) one cohesive behavior area per *.test.ts file; (2) shared fixtures/helpers live in exactly ONE co-located *-support.ts module per subsystem — never duplicated across test files; (3) descriptive describe labels naming the behavior area; (4) a soft size budget (~600 lines / ~25 tests) that TRIGGERS a split into themed files rather than a hard cap.

Two decisions for you:
  A. Do these 4 rules match what you want, or would you add/cut/change any?
  B. Where should it live, given the priority says 'CoCoder AND the repos it manages'? My recommendation: a base shared standard (packages/personas/base/standards/test-architecture.md) so it binds every managed workspace, authored through this run's verify gate with the relevant persona/standards tests. The alternative is a CoCoder-only doc (cocoder/standards/ or docs/) that binds only this repo. I recommend the base shared standard.

Once you confirm A and B, the concrete next atom is to author that standard doc (and, if you want, a follow-up to apply rule 2's single-owner pattern to the remaining large-but-cohesive files like tickets.test.ts). What's your call?

Scope: 2 of 8 workspace projects

data.task:
Phase 2 of 'Review test architecture and componentize': author the founder-approved unit-test architecture standard as a NEW governance doc. Create exactly one file: cocoder/standards/test-architecture.md (this is the CoCoder governance overlay that travels with each managed root — NOT engine package source). Do not touch any other file except, if and only if cocoder/standards/ contains an index/README that lists the standards, add one pointer line to it for the new doc.

The doc must state, clearly and concisely (this is governance prose, keep it tight, conclusion-first):

Purpose: the unit-test architecture standard for CoCoder and the repos it manages, going forward.

Four rules:
1. One cohesive behavior area per *.test.ts file. A file tests one area; unrelated concerns get their own file.
2. Shared fixtures/helpers live in exactly ONE co-located *-support.ts module per subsystem (the single owner). Never copy a fixture/helper across test files — import it from the support module.
3. Each test file wraps its tests in a descriptive `describe` label naming the behavior area.
4. A SOFT size budget (~600 lines / ~25 tests) TRIGGERS a split into themed files — it is a prompt to break up, not a hard cap. Cohesive files may exceed it with reason.

Test location rule (founder-directed, state it explicitly):
- Tests live INSIDE the root, alongside the code they test, run by that root's own test runner — e.g. a package's tests/ dir or co-located *.test.ts next to the source.
- Tests NEVER live inside [root]/cocoder/. That directory is exclusively the CoCoder governance overlay (priorities, tickets, standards, personas, decisions). The standard PRESCRIBES where tests go (next to the code); it does not HOUSE them.
- Tests travel with the root because they travel with the code.

Dogfooding (state it, with CoCoder's real paths): CoCoder's own engine tests live in packages/*/tests/ (e.g. packages/core/tests/runner-*.test.ts importing ../src), and CoCoder's governance — including this standard — lives in CoCoder/cocoder/. A managed root keeps its tests next to its code and its CoCoder overlay in [root]/cocoder/.

Adapt-first rule (founder-directed, state it explicitly as its own short clause): when CoCoder takes over an EXISTING repo that already has its own test conventions, follow that repo's conventions — do not force CoCoder's. This standard is the DEFAULT for new test work and a convergence target over time, never a forced top-down migration of an existing layout. (Deeper detection/honoring of a taken-over repo's conventions is tracked in ticket 0089.)

Worked example to cite briefly: run_294 split packages/core/tests/runner.test.ts (5,638 lines, one monolithic describe) into 17 focused files plus one shared runner.test-support.ts fixture owner, behavior-preserving (741 tests green) — the standard codifies that pattern.

Acceptance criteria:
- cocoder/standards/test-architecture.md exists and contains all four rules, the test-location rule, the adapt-first rule, and the dogfooding paragraph.
- `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core test` stays GREEN (this is a docs-only change; it must not alter any test) and `pnpm -r typecheck` (or the core typecheck) stays green — show evidence.
- No file other than cocoder/standards/test-architecture.md (and at most one standards-index pointer line) is changed.

Write scope: cocoder/standards/** (declared writePath: cocoder/standards/test-architecture.md). This is off Bob's usual packages/** lane — that is expected and fine (scope is advisory, ADR-0045); the runner will commit and flag it. Report the created file path and the test/typecheck evidence.
